name: epc-qr

Use when asked to: add a QR code to a bill, generate a payment QR code, create a Girocode, embed an EPC QR code in an ODT invoice, show a QR code for SEPA payment, add a payment QR image to a bill template."

---

# Generating EPC QR Codes in TwelveLittleScoutsClerk

An **EPC QR code** (also called *Girocode*) encodes all data needed to initiate a SEPA Credit Transfer. It is standardised by the European Payments Council (EPC guideline v2.10).

Full format specification: [epc-format.md](./references/epc-format.md)  
Java helper template: [EpcQrHelper.java](./references/EpcQrHelper.java)

---

## Step 1 — Add ZXing dependency to `pom.xml`

ZXing ("Zebra Crossing") is the standard Java QR library. Add inside `<dependencies>`:

```xml
<!-- ZXing QR code generation -->
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.google.zxing</groupId>
    <artifactId>core</artifactId>
    <version>3.5.3</version>
</dependency>
<dependency>
    <groupId>com.google.zxing</groupId>
    <artifactId>javase</artifactId>
    <version>3.5.3</version>
</dependency>
```

Then run `mvn compile` to download and verify the dependency resolves.

---

## Step 2 — Create `EpcQrHelper.java`

**File:** `src/at/redeye/twelvelittlescoutsclerk/EpcQrHelper.java`  
**Package:** `at.redeye.twelvelittlescoutsclerk`

Copy and adapt the template from [EpcQrHelper.java](./references/EpcQrHelper.java).

Key method signatures:

```java
// Build the EPC payload string
public static String buildEpcPayload(String beneficiaryName, String iban,
        String bic, double amountEur, String remittanceUnstructured,
        String purposeCode)

// Render to a BufferedImage (for display or embedding)
public static BufferedImage generateQrImage(String epcPayload, int sizePixels)
        throws WriterException

// Convenience: build payload from AppConfigDefinitions + amount/ref
public static BufferedImage generateBillQr(double amountEur, String remittance)
        throws WriterException
```

### Important rules
- Beneficiary name: max 70 characters
- IBAN: strip spaces before encoding (raw alphanumeric only)
- BIC: optional in version 002 (leave empty string `""` to omit)
- Amount: format as `EUR` + decimal with dot, e.g. `EUR12.34`; `EUR0` means "unspecified"
- Remittance info: either structured (`/ISO/...`) **or** unstructured text, not both; max 140 chars
- Character set line must match the actual encoding; use UTF-8 (value `1`)
- QR error correction: **M** (`ErrorCorrectionLevel.M`) as required by EPC spec
- Maximum payload: 331 bytes in UTF-8

---

## Step 3 — Using the QR code

### A. Display in a Swing dialog

```java
BufferedImage img = EpcQrHelper.generateBillQr(event_member.costs.getValue(), "Rechnung " + bill.billingnr.getValue());
ImageIcon icon = new ImageIcon(img);
JLabel qrLabel = new JLabel(icon);
```

### B. Embed in an ODT bill template

The image placeholder in the ODT must be a `draw:frame` with `draw:name="qr_code"` (or `"Bild2"` as fallback — the current name in `billing_template.odt`). `BillingHelper.injectEpcQrCode()` finds this frame automatically and replaces the image bytes in the ODF package at the existing `xlink:href` path.

### C. Save QR image to file (download from UI)

```java
File out = new File(chooser.getSelectedFile() + ".png");
ImageIO.write(img, "PNG", out);
```

---

## Step 4 — Add BIC to AppConfigDefinitions (if needed)

EPC version 001 requires BIC; version 002 makes it optional (recommended to omit for SEPA-only).  
If BIC is needed, add to `AppConfigDefinitions.java`:

```java
public static DBConfig OrganisationBIC = new DBConfig("OrganisationBIC", "", "BIC der Organisation (optional)");
```

Then `add(OrganisationBIC)` in the constructor and access via:

```java
AppConfigDefinitions.OrganisationBIC.getConfigValue()
```

---

## Checklist

- [ ] ZXing `core` + `javase` added to `pom.xml` and `mvn compile` succeeds
- [ ] `EpcQrHelper.java` created in the right package
- [ ] IBAN sourced from `AppConfigDefinitions.OrganisaiontIBAN.getConfigValue()` with spaces stripped
- [ ] Beneficiary name from `AppConfigDefinitions.Organisation.getConfigValue()`
- [ ] Amount formatted as `EUR` + decimal (no trailing zeros beyond 2 dp, or omit for "any amount")
- [ ] QR error correction level set to `M`
- [ ] Every new class/method marked `// generated by AI (Claude Sonnet 4.6)`
